behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the heuristic algorithm itself (single in-process computation). No OS/network/subprocess dependency, no AI/ML output dependency, and no multi-agent sequence. Higher levels were considered and rejected because the behavior text is strictly about route-length quality of the heuristic for bounded inputs, not service wiring or external calls.
behaviors.test_description: Call the heuristic function directly with deterministic coordinate sets (n<=20), compute exact optimal tour in-test (e.g., brute force for small n / Held-Karp for up to 20), and assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic arithmetic/rule application (distance and speed mapping) at function/object boundary. No real infrastructure required, no model output, no multi-step agent pipeline. Integration was rejected because behavior text focuses on correctness of computation, not inter-module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the time-estimation function with fixed segment distances and road-type speed config; assert per-segment and total window calculations match expected values, including rounding/units edge cases.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in local validation logic: compare totals and compute excess. No OS/network/subprocess, no AI/ML judgment, no chained agentic steps. Integration/System were rejected because behavior text is pure business-rule validation.
behaviors.test_description: Call validator with route_total_time and remaining_shift_hours fixtures across boundary conditions (equal, just over, far over); assert rejection flag and exact excess amount.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module boundaries: geocoding-result handling + skip logic + optimizer input + reporting output in one service flow. A unit test of any single function can miss handoff/state bugs. System was considered and rejected because behavior text only requires handling the API semantic outcome ('returns no results'), not real HTTP/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with in-process geocoding client stub returning coordinates for N-1 addresses and empty result for 1 address; assert skipped-address report contains that address, optimizer receives only valid stops, and returned route excludes skipped stop.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: Minimum real failure boundary is preprocessing-to-routing handoff: deduplication/merge must propagate into route construction so stop is visited once while items are combined. Unit-only tests can miss cross-module state propagation issues. System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement and no AI/ML decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute service pipeline with duplicate-address inputs carrying different items; assert output stop list has one consolidated stop for that address with merged items and final route contains that stop exactly once.
